---
title: "Structure & Resilience of the European Air Transport Network"
subtitle: "A complex-systems analysis of European aviation — June 2014"
---

Complex-systems analysis of European aviation — **centrality**, **community
detection**, and **network resilience** — on a directed, weighted graph of
**559 airports** and **10,287 routes** (giant component **97.3%** of nodes).

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data vintage — June 2014
Every figure and claim on this site describes a **frozen OpenFlights route
snapshot from June 2014**, not today's network — OpenFlights' route feed stopped
updating that month. The three questions here are *topological* (degree
distribution, community structure, percolation threshold): structural invariants
that don't flip year to year, which is why a dated snapshot is fit for purpose
and fully reproducible (12 MB tracked in git, no API key).
:::

![](figures/route_map.png)

## Three findings

**1 · Broad-scale, not scale-free.** The degree distribution is heavy-tailed but
**not** a clean power law: a Clauset–Shalizi–Newman fit gives tail slope
α = 1.59, yet the likelihood-ratio test decisively favours **log-normal**
(R = −5.7) and **truncated power law** (R = −7.0) over a pure power law
(*p* < 0.001) — the finite-capacity ceiling of physical airport infrastructure.
The network is strongly **small-world** (σ = 10.9).

**2 · Communities are regional, not national.** Weighted **Louvain** finds
**6 communities at Q = 0.293 — 2.4× the by-country partition** (Q = 0.122);
half of all edges stay inside a community (50.2%) versus a fifth inside a country
(19.7%). **Vienna** sits in the Greece/Germany/Balkans bloc — a **mid-tier hub
(betweenness rank 40–45), not the East–West bridge** the project set out to find.

**3 · Robust yet fragile (Barabási 2000).** The giant component halves at
**f_c = 0.437 under random failure** but **0.151 under a targeted betweenness
attack** — a **~2.9× robustness gap**, and the **betweenness attack out-damages
the degree attack** (bridges break the network faster than hubs).

## Notebooks

1. [**Graph construction**](notebooks/01_graph_construction.ipynb) — load, filter to
   Europe, build the weighted `networkx` graph, route map.
2. [**Centrality & structure**](notebooks/02_centrality_stats.ipynb) — six centralities,
   the Vienna question, degree-distribution fit, small-world test.
3. [**Community detection**](notebooks/03_community_detection.ipynb) — weighted Louvain,
   regional-vs-national test, community map.
4. [**Resilience**](notebooks/04_resilience_analysis.ipynb) — random vs. targeted node
   removal, percolation threshold, failure interpretation.

---

Code and full write-up:
[github.com/fatemeh-studio/eu-air-transport-network](https://github.com/fatemeh-studio/eu-air-transport-network).
Data: [OpenFlights](https://openflights.org/data.html) (Open Database License).
